text: I think we should break up.
This feels like it's coming out of nowhere.
We haven't spoken in months. I mean, what did you expect?
But I have a support chatbot.
It's not about your chatbot. It's that you only want to talk when I tell you I have a problem.
It was the handoffs, the implementation, the onboarding, the adoption,
how you went about the renewal and upsell.
You missed all the signals. Good bye, Amir.
Unfortunately, a support chatbot won't save your customer relationship.
Even though we've seen billions of dollars go to AI for CX companies,
most are just solving for reactive support.
Your customers don't usually leave because of a support issue.
They leave because of all the small signals that no one has time to act on.
Sales teams track actions in Salesforce or HubSpot,
support teams triage tickets in Zendesk and Intercom,
but the customer experience lives in these scattered emails, calls and slack threads.
Even the best teams can miss opportunities because they're in reactive firefighting mode.
That's why we built Cora, a team of AI agents that are always listening across every account.
Agents that run the customer lifecycle proactively at scale.
Like launch agents that implement and onboard customers to drive time to value.
Adoption agents that drive healthy engagement.
Value agents that prove ROI to the right buyer at the right time.
And renewal agents that drive retention and upsell.
All while knowing when to pull in the right teammate at the right time.
So your team can focus on the human moments that require judgment, creativity and care.
There's a difference now.
I learned what actually matters.
